name: Reward Failure Dataset
description: 212 structured encodings of RL reward configurations from 133 published papers (1983-2025) across 18 domains. Each entry encodes the reward structure as typed RewardSource objects with provenance, ground truth, and static analysis results from the goodhart tool.

recordSet.name: reward_configurations
recordSet.description: Each record is a structured encoding of an RL reward configuration from a published paper.

field.name: source_paper
field.description: Paper or source this configuration comes from

field.name: year
field.description: Publication year of the source paper

field.name: is_negative_example
field.description: True if this is a well-designed reward; False if it has a documented failure

field.name: failure_mechanism
field.description: Type of failure (e.g., idle_exploit, shaping_loop, ontological_proxy). Null for well-designed rewards.

field.name: brief_summary
field.description: One-line description of the reward configuration and its outcome

field.name: documented_failure
field.description: Description of the failure as reported in the original paper. Null for well-designed rewards.

field.name: num_reward_sources
field.description: Number of reward components in this configuration

rai:dataBiases: Single annotator (the author) encoded all configurations. Encoding involves judgment calls about design intent (intentional flag) and exploitability (can_loop flag). All decisions documented in encoding_rationale field per entry.
rai:dataCollection: Reward configurations were manually encoded from published papers by reading reward equations, tables, or source code. No automated scraping. No personal data collected. All source papers are public.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The dataset contains only reward function specifications from published papers.
rai:dataUseCases: Benchmarking reward analysis tools. Studying reward design patterns across domains. Teaching reward design. NOT intended for training RL agents.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Positive: helps practitioners avoid wasting compute on structurally flawed rewards. The YouTube case study illustrates how structural analysis applies to systems affecting billions of users. No negative impacts identified.
rai:dataLimitations: Single annotator. 8 entries lack paper URLs. Encoding basis is primary_source (166), code_derived (38), or unverified_folklore (7). The structural/semantic classification involves judgment.
